{"id":"project-LICHEN-bvdu","title":"Router.pending_queue allows unbounded destinations causing memory exhaustion","description":"file: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:353-356

Router.pending_queue limits packets per destination (max_pending_per_dest=3) but places no limit on the number of destinations. An attacker or malfunctioning network could send packets to many unique destinations, causing unbounded memory growth.

The _queue_pending method (line 515-541) adds new destination keys without checking total destination count. While expire_pending removes old entries, continuous addition of new destinations faster than expiration could exhaust memory.

Suggested fix: Add max_pending_destinations limit and evict oldest destination when exceeded, similar to ForwardingBuffer's max_sources pattern.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:02:24.044629Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:02:24.044658Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-bvdu","title":"Router.pending_queue allows unbounded destinations causing memory exhaustion","description":"file: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:353-356

Router.pending_queue limits packets per destination (max_pending_per_dest=3) but places no limit on the number of destinations. An attacker or malfunctioning network could send packets to many unique destinations, causing unbounded memory growth.

The _queue_pending method (line 515-541) adds new destination keys without checking total destination count. While expire_pending removes old entries, continuous addition of new destinations faster than expiration could exhaust memory.

Suggested fix: Add max_pending_destinations limit and evict oldest destination when exceeded, similar to ForwardingBuffer's max_sources pattern.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:02:24.044629Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T22:55:11.417198214Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T22:55:11.417198214Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T23:00:11.417198214Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T22:55:11.417198214Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Claimed; analyzed router.py and test_router.py; identified need to implement max_pending_destinations with _pending_order LRU like neighbors/ForwardingBuffer"}

old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-bvdu","title":"Router.pending_queue allows unbounded destinations causing memory exhaustion","description":"file: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:353-356

Router.pending_queue limits packets per destination (max_pending_per_dest=3) but places no limit on the number of destinations. An attacker or malfunctioning network could send packets to many unique destinations, causing unbounded memory growth.

The _queue_pending method (line 515-541) adds new destination keys without checking total destination count. While expire_pending removes old entries, continuous addition of new destinations faster than expiration could exhaust memory.

Suggested fix: Add max_pending_destinations limit and evict oldest destination when exceeded, similar to ForwardingBuffer's max_sources pattern.","notes":"Claimed; analyzed router.py and test_router.py; identified need to implement max_pending_destinations with _pending_order LRU like neighbors/ForwardingBuffer","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:02:24.044629Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T22:56:37.672750336Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T22:55:11.417198214Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T23:00:11.417198214Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T22:55:11.417198214Z"}
